title: Sixteen Hundred Strangers

mood.label: Heavy going 🌧️
mood.summary: A heavier day, with Venezuela's quake dead climbing past fourteen hundred under a peaking European heat dome and Africa's crises, but with a whole world's worth of rescuers already digging at the rubble.

style.genre: motorik / krautrock — a sampled Rhodes-ish electric piano laying patient dorian chords over a steady, driving four-on-the-floor pulse, with a sampled vibraphone and a high flute tracing a circling topline through tape-echo, a plucked upright bass walking underneath and a sine sub holding the floor; E dorian's raised sixth keeping the minor from collapsing into grief — forward motion under a heavy sky, like a relief column that just keeps moving

sampleSources: Strudel default library (sampled piano, VCSL vibraphone/flute, RolandTR909, EmuSP12)
pattern:
stack(
  s("bd ~ ~ bd ~ ~ bd ~, ~ ~ hh ~ ~ ~ hh ~, ~ ~ ~ ~ rim ~ ~ ~").bank("RolandTR909").gain(0.62).hpf(120),
  s("hh*8").bank("EmuSP12").gain(perlin.range(0.08,0.22).slow(7)).pan(sine.range(0.35,0.65).slow(5)),
  note("<[e2 e2 b2 e2] [a1 a1 e2 a2] [cs2 cs2 gs2 cs3] [b1 b1 fs2 b2]>").s("piano").gain(0.34).lpf(700).sustain(0.4).room(0.18),
  note("<[e3,g3,b3] [a3,cs4,e4] [cs3,e3,g3] [b3,d4,fs4]>").s("piano").attack(0.18).sustain(0.6).release(1.4).lpf(sine.range(900,2200).slow(12)).gain(0.26).room(0.32).pan(0.42),
  note("e4 ~ g4 b4 ~ a4 fs4 ~ ~ e4 d4 ~ b3 ~ cs4 ~").s("vibraphone").gain(0.3).delay(0.32).delaytime(0.27).delayfeedback(0.34).room(0.3).pan(0.6),
  note("<~ b5 ~ ~> <e5 ~ ~ d5> <~ ~ g5 ~> <fs5 ~ ~ ~>").s("flute").gain(0.22).attack(0.08).release(0.9).lpf(3200).delay(0.25).delaytime(0.375).room(0.4).pan(0.3),
  note("e1 ~ e1 b1 a1 ~ a1 e1").s("sine").lpf(420).gain(0.4).sustain(0.5)
)

lines: Four days under the concrete now, and still they call the names,
the morgues are full in La Guaira, the lists are full of blanks.
But sixteen hundred strangers came with shovels and with light —
they don't speak the language here, they dig in it all night.
Heavy sky, heavy ground, keep the column moving on,
the heat won't break, the fevers spread, and still the trucks roll on.
Count the grief, then count the hands that crossed the world to come —
the sum is hard, the sum is hard, but it doesn't add to none.
Vienna burns at thirty-nine, the rivers take the young,
a convoy falls near El Fasher with the famine bell unrung.
Yet plants grow medicine in the dark, the quiet cars outsell the loud —
small green proofs the day still works beneath the heavy cloud.
